member.name: Florence Eshalomi
member.role: MP for Vauxhall and Camberwell Green
member.synopsis: Florence Eshalomi is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Vauxhall and Camberwell Green , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS: Drugs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment his Department has made of the effectiveness of UK efforts to increase the global availability of medicines to prevent HIV.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK remains committed to sustainable development goal 3.3 including ending AIDS by 2030. The UK supports partners including UNAIDS, Unitaid, and the Global Fund to improve equitable access to HIV prevention services tailored to individual needs. In 202...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve (a) gender parity, (b) equitable investment and (c) the focus on women for HIV (i) prevention, (ii) research, (iii) data and (iv) services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are committed to ensuring that everyone benefits equally from HIV prevention, treatment, and care, and the Department, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA), NHS England, and a broad range of system partners are working together to develop a new HIV Ac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS: Vaccination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what funding and resources are being allocated to support ongoing clinical trials and research for HIV vaccines within the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department funds research through the National Institute of Health and Care Research (NIHR). The NIHR funds clinical research into HIV as well as vaccine development, however, it is not currently funding any trials on HIV vaccines. The NIHR welcomes r...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS: Vaccination
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to increase the speed of the (a) development and (b) approval of effective HIV vaccines.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is committed to maximising the United Kingdom’s potential to lead the world in clinical research, with the aim of ensuring that all patients, including those with human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), have access to cutting-edge clinical tria...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Housing Allowance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of local housing allowance rates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Local Housing Allowance (LHA) rates are reviewed annually, usually in the Autumn. LHA determines the maximum housing support for tenants in the private rented sector and do not cover all rents in all areas. The Secretary of State’s decision to maintain LH...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to expand access to preventative HIV care beyond sexual health services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring equality and equity of access to HIV prevention and treatment for all. We recognise that testing is the best way to determine your HIV status, and to ensure people can take appropriate measures to protect themselves...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS: Health Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to help ensure HIV health promotion campaigns are (a) culturally appropriate and (b) effective in reaching (i) under-served and (ii) at-risk communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring equality and equity of access to HIV prevention and treatment for all. We have commissioned officials for advice on how to progress the development of a new HIV Action Plan, which we aim to publish by summer 2025. T...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to (a) tackle inequalities in access to preventative HIV care and (b) end new HIV transmissions by 2030.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HIV is a key priority for the Government, and we have commissioned officials for advice on how to progress the development of a new HIV Action Plan to end new HIV transmissions within England by 2030, with a strong focus on tackling inequalities, HIV care...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to support local commissioning areas to (a) identify and (b) address HIV inequalities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HIV is a key priority for the Government, and we have commissioned officials for advice on how to progress the development of a new HIV Action Plan to end new HIV transmissions within England by 2030, with a strong focus on tackling inequalities, HIV care...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the implications for his policies of levels of inequality in HIV (a) care access and (b) outcomes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HIV is a key priority for the Government, and we have commissioned officials for advice on how to progress the development of a new HIV Action Plan to end new HIV transmissions within England by 2030, with a strong focus on tackling inequalities, HIV care...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when his Department plans to publish a new HIV Action Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HIV is a key priority for the Government, and we have commissioned officials for advice on how to progress the development of a new HIV Action Plan to end new HIV transmissions within England by 2030, with a strong focus on tackling inequalities, HIV care...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Democratic Republic of Congo: Mpox
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assistance his Department is giving to the Democratic Republic of the Congo for the recent mpox outbreaks in that country.
writtenQuestions.answerText: During his visit to DRC in August, the Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State (Africa), Lord Collins, announced £3.1 million UK funding to UNICEF for cholera and mpox response in DRC. It will reach 4.4 million people in affected communities and will boost...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Gonorrhoea and Mpox: Vaccination

writtenQuestions.answerText: On 10 November 2023 the Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation (JCVI) issued advice on creating targeted, routine vaccination programmes for the prevention of Mpox and gonorrhoea.Officials across the Department, the UK Health Security Agency, and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: On 10 November 2023 the Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation (JCVI) issued advice on creating targeted, routine vaccination programmes for the prevention of Mpox and gonorrhoea.Officials across the Department, the UK Health Security Agency, and...

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to help reduce the number of HIV transmissions to zero by 2030.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I would like to congratulate my Honourable Friend for her re-election and for her valuable work as Co-Chair of the All-Party Parliamentary Group on HIV, AIDS and Sexual Health in the last parliament, as well as her advocacy on HIV, particularly for the Bl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Elections: Proof of Identity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities, whether he plans to amend the list of acceptable photo ID before the next General Election.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to https://www.gov.uk/government/consultations/supporting-our-veterans-a-consultation. Further policy will be set out in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how much funding her Department plans to provide for research and development into HIV in the (a) 2024-25 and (b) 2025-26 financial year.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department supports research and development on the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) through the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR). For the financial years 2024/25 and 2025/26, the Government has committed to a new £20 million r...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HIV/AIDS: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how much funding her Department has provided for research and development into a (a) cure and (b) vaccine for HIV in each year since 2019.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department supports research on the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) through the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR). The remit of the NIHR is translational, clinical, and applied healthcare research. Basic research towards the de...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: International Law: HIV/AIDS
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister of State, Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office, whether his Department is providing support to (a) the HIV Justice Network and (b) other organisations working to repeal HIV-specific laws globally.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Addressing stigma, discrimination and criminalisation is critical to ensuring equality of access to HIV prevention, testing and treatment services and to achieving progress in the global HIV response.The UK is a champion of human rights around the world a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Commonwealth: HIV/AIDS
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister of State, Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office, what steps he is taking to help encourage the repeal of HIV specific laws in the 20 jurisdictions in the Commonwealth that still have them.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Addressing stigma, discrimination and criminalisation is critical to ensuring equality of access to HIV prevention, testing and treatment services and to achieving progress in the global HIV response.The UK is a champion of human rights around the world a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

items.text: Name of donor: Doha Forum Address of donor: Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Almirqab Tower, West Bay, Doha Qatar Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£5555.00), accommodation and food (£402.00), value £5,957 Destination of visit: Qatar (Doha) Dates of visit: 5 December 2025 to 8 December 2025 Purpose of visit: To participate in the annual Doha Forum 2025 on diplomacy, dialogue and diversity, including meetings with fellow cross-party parliamentarians, political representatives of other countries and Qatari ministers to discuss Qatar's role in seeking to resolve current regional conflicts. (Registered 19 December 2025)
